top_mean_weight_agent.clinical_interpretation: temporal-spatial progression and longitudinal change evidence

top_individual_auc_agent.clinical_interpretation: localized tissue-density and regional pattern evidence

evidence_table.interpretability_question: Which clinical pathway most strongly influenced organizational inference?
evidence_table.quantitative_evidence: TemporalSpatialAgent had the highest mean adaptive weight (0.2260).
evidence_table.clinical_interpretation: temporal-spatial progression and longitudinal change evidence
evidence_table.safe_claim: Adaptive influence weights provide pathway-level explanation of the model decision process.

evidence_table.interpretability_question: Which individual pathway had the strongest standalone discrimination?
evidence_table.quantitative_evidence: LocalRegionalAgent had the highest individual-agent ROC-AUC (0.7053).
evidence_table.clinical_interpretation: localized tissue-density and regional pattern evidence
evidence_table.safe_claim: Individual pathway performance helps identify which clinical reasoning stream carried the strongest independent signal.

evidence_table.interpretability_question: Which pathway showed the clearest positive marginal contribution?
evidence_table.quantitative_evidence: Removing BilateralAgent produced the largest positive ROC-AUC loss (0.0309).
evidence_table.clinical_interpretation: left-right breast asymmetry and structural difference evidence
evidence_table.safe_claim: Leave-one-role-out evidence supports a measurable contribution for this pathway in the equal-weight fusion setting.

evidence_table.interpretability_question: Which pathway was most frequently dominant at the case level?
evidence_table.quantitative_evidence: TemporalSpatialAgent was dominant in 890 cases (0.8900 of test cases).
evidence_table.clinical_interpretation: temporal-spatial progression and longitudinal change evidence
evidence_table.safe_claim: Case-level dominance provides an interpretable summary of which diagnostic pathway controlled inference most often.

evidence_table.interpretability_question: Does this replace radiologist-annotated saliency validation?
evidence_table.quantitative_evidence: No lesion-mask Dice/IoU analysis was performed because the SACU framework is descriptor/pathway-based.
evidence_table.clinical_interpretation: Interpretability is provided at the diagnostic-pathway level, not at pixel-level heatmap localization.
evidence_table.safe_claim: The manuscript should state this limitation clearly and avoid claiming expert-validated lesion localization.

important_limitation: This analysis provides organizational/pathway-level interpretability, not pixel-level lesion-mask saliency validation.
